body.title: [sprint] Backfill intentions store from chat logs (Haiku scan of 'intent' -> classify keepers)
body.intention: I intend that the intentions Akien has expressed deliberately over weeks of chat are recovered into the store rather than lost to scrollback.
body.description:
**Problem:** Akien has used the words 'intent'/'intention' deliberately (with intent) for several weeks, so those mentions are high-signal intention statements scattered across chat history. They need recovering into the new intentions store.

**Source location (verified 2026-06-30):** the canonical chat history is NOT in memory/chat.cc.0 or claude_chat_logs/ (both empty, .gitkeep only) — it is the ~375 harness transcripts at ~/.claude/projects/-home-akien-dev-src-UnseenUniversity/*.jsonl. Scan those (or run /export-chat --all first to materialize markdown, then scan).

**Proposed shape:** Delegatable Haiku sweep (feedback_delegate_mechanical_sweeps): (1) case-insensitive grep 'intent' across the JSONL transcripts; (2) extract each hit with surrounding context; (3) Haiku-classify each candidate into keeper / not-an-intention; (4) normalize keepers into 'I intend that...' statements, dedup; (5) emit as I-<slug> records into the intentions store; (6) HUMAN-REVIEW checkpoint — Akien confirms the keeper set before final commit (the evaluation pass); (7) CONFLICT RECONCILIATION — where extracted intentions conflict with each other or with existing intentions, run a joint Akien+CC reconciliation pass (intentions compete/merge/decompose — project_intentions_as_living_entities) rather than auto-resolving.

**Affected files:** a one-shot script under devlab/claudecode/ (e.g. extract_intentions.py); writes to devlab/runtime/memory/intentions/.
**Design rules:** emit via memory_emit.py only; no SQLite; surface dropped/ambiguous candidates (do not silently truncate — reference_sweep_guard).
**Scope boundary:** extraction + classification + emit + Akien review. Do not merge/decompose intentions yet; do not delete source chat logs.
**Completion criteria:** a candidate list is produced from the JSONL scan (count logged), classified keepers are emitted as schema-valid intention records, and the dropped/ambiguous set is reported for Akien review. Prove via: emitted records are schema-valid and N_keepers logged with the candidate count it was filtered from.
**Test plan:** likely shipped-unproven on the curation judgment (no clean red form for 'is this a real intention'); the mechanical half IS provable — assert every emitted record validates against the intention envelope and that candidate->keeper counts are both logged.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
